words.def: a living thing that grows in the earth and usually has a stem, leaves and roots, especially one that is smaller than a tree or bush
words.example: All plants need light and water.

words.def: made of plastic
a flat, usually round, dish that you put food on
used as a polite way of asking for something or telling somebody to do something
feeling happy about something
a small piece of material like a small bag sewn into or onto a piece of clothing so that you can carry things in it
an official organization whose job is to make people obey the law and to prevent and solve crime; the people who work for this organization
words.example: Get out of the house or I'll call the police.

words.def: a male police officer
having or showing good manners and respect for the feelings of others
words.example: polite to somebody, Please be polite to our guests.

words.def: the process of making air, water, soil, etc. dirty; the state of being dirty
liked or enjoyed by a large number of people
words.example: Andalucia is a popular tourist destination.

words.def: all the people who live in a particular area, city or country; the total number of people who live there
words.example: One third of the world’s population consumes/consume two thirds of the world’s resources.

words.def: that can be done or achieved
words.example: possible to do something, New technology has made it possible to communicate more easily.

words.def: a large notice, often with a picture on it, that is put in a public place to advertise something
to say that something will happen in the future
words.example: predict something, a reliable method of predicting earthquakes

words.def: to like one thing or person better than another; to choose one thing rather than something else because you like it better
words.example: prefer something, ‘Coffee or tea?’ ‘I'd prefer tea, thanks.’

words.def: to make something or somebody ready to be used or to do something
words.example: prepare something/somebody, to prepare a report

words.def: to stop somebody from doing something; to stop something from happening
words.example: prevent something/somebody, The accident could have been prevented.

words.def: a machine for printing text on paper, especially one connected to a computer
used to say that something is likely to happen or to be true
a thing that is grown, produced or created, usually for sale
a statement that tells somebody that you will definitely do or not do something
to make the sound of a word or letter in a particular way
to give something to somebody or make it available for them to use
words.example: provide something, Please provide the following information.

words.def: to produce a book, magazine, CD-ROM, etc. and sell it to the public
having the colour of blue and red mixed together
the intention, aim or function of something; the thing that something is supposed to achieve
words.example: Our campaign's main purpose is to raise money.

words.def: the female ruler of an independent state that has a royal family
words.example: She was crowned queen at the age of fifteen.

words.def: a sentence, phrase or word that asks for information
done with speed; taking or lasting a short time
in a way that makes very little noise
to some degree
to lift or move something to a higher level
words.example: She raised the gun and fired.

words.def: used to mean ‘fairly’ or ‘to some degree’, often when you are disappointed, surprised or expressing slight criticism
to understand or become aware of a particular fact or situation
words.example: realize (that)…, I didn’t realize (that) you were so unhappy.

words.def: to get or accept something that is sent or given to you
that happened or began only a short time ago
not long ago
to treat things that have already been used so that they can be used again
to send somebody/something to somebody/something for help, advice or a decision
words.example: refer somebody/something to somebody/something, My doctor referred me to a specialist.

words.def: following a pattern, especially with the same time or space in between each thing and the next
to rest while you are doing something that you enjoy, especially after work or effort
words.example: Just relax and enjoy the movie.

words.def: to be used instead of somebody/something else; to do something instead of somebody/something else
words.example: The new design will eventually replace all existing models.

words.def: an act of replying to something/somebody in speech, writing or by some action
words.example: I asked her what her name was but she made no reply.

words.def: a written or spoken account of an event, especially one that is published or broadcast
a person who collects and reports news for newspapers, radio or television
words.example: He leaked the story to a news reporter.

words.def: a careful study of a subject, especially in order to discover new facts or information about it
a spoken or written answer
the action of arriving in or coming back to a place that you were in before
words.example: return of somebody to…, A week had passed since their return to Geneva.

words.def: a report in a newspaper or magazine, or on the internet, television or radio, in which somebody gives their opinion of a book, play, film, product, etc.; the act of writing this kind of report
a short journey in a vehicle, on a bicycle, etc.
words.example: a train ride through beautiful countryside

words.def: a piece of jewellery that you wear on your finger, consisting of a round band of gold, silver, etc., sometimes decorated with precious stones
a hard surface built for vehicles to travel on
an actor’s part in a play, film, etc.
words.example: She has landed the lead role (= the most important one) in a new play.

words.def: the structure that covers or forms the top of a building or vehicle
having or showing a lack of respect for other people and their feelings
an act of running; a period of time spent running or the distance that somebody runs
words.example: I go for a run every morning.

words.def: a person or an animal that runs, especially one taking part in a race
the action or sport of running
in a sad way
protected from any danger, harm or loss
words.example: The children are quite safe here.

words.def: the sport or activity of travelling in a boat with sails
an act or the process of selling something
words.example: regulations governing the sale of alcoholic beverages

words.def: a white substance that is added to food to make it taste better or to preserve it. Salt is obtained from mines and is also found in seawater. It is sometimes called common salt to show that it is different from other chemical salts. Its chemical name is sodium chloride.
a thick liquid that is eaten with food to add taste to it
to keep somebody/something safe from death, harm, loss, etc.
words.example: save somebody/something, to save somebody’s life

words.def: the place where something happens, especially something unpleasant
an attempt to find somebody/something, especially by looking carefully for them/it
words.example: search for somebody/something, a long search for the murder weapon

words.def: after one other person or thing in order or importance
words.example: She came second in the marathon.

words.def: a person who works in an office, working for another person, dealing with mail and phone calls, keeping records, arranging meetings with people, etc.
to give the impression of being or doing something
to make something go or be taken to a place, especially by post, email, radio, etc.
words.example: send something, She sent a letter of support.

words.def: to give somebody food or drink, for example at a restaurant or during a meal
words.example: serve (something), Breakfast is served between 7 and 10 a.m.

words.def: more than two but not very many
used with I and we for talking about or predicting the future
words.example: This time next week I shall be in Scotland.

words.def: either of the two parts of the body between the top of each arm and the neck
a loud cry of anger, fear, excitement, etc.
a programme on television or the radio
words.example: Millions of people watch the show.

words.def: either of the two halves of a surface, an object or an area that is divided by an imaginary central line
words.example: the right side of the brain

words.def: an event, an action, a fact, etc. that shows that something exists, is happening or may happen in the future
words.example: Looking back, I should have seen the signs.

words.def: shiny grey-white in colour
like somebody/something but not exactly the same
not complicated; easy to understand or do
the activity of making musical sounds with your voice
